behavior_summary: This script implements the object's 'U8P_CLOTH::look' (examine) handler. When the player examines the object, the game displays a single short text/bark "cloth" and then the process suspends/ends.
There are no menus, branches, global flags, or external function calls referenced; the behavior is purely descriptive (display text) and terminates immediately after.
The routine is registered as a process (processType 0x0208) and therefore is executed by the engine when the object is inspected.
notable_control_flow: U8P_CLOTH::look starts as a process triggered by a U8P_CLOTH::look/examine action.
The process emits a Bark node with the string "cloth".
Immediately after the bark the process issues a suspend (process termination/pause).
open_questions: Is this item referenced by other scripts or game logic for contextual descriptions or inventory (no evidence in this file) — low confidence.
Does the engine treat the trailing 'suspend' as a hard termination of the U8P_CLOTH::look process or as a yield that can be resumed by other systems? (inferred as termination; medium confidence).

main_function.name: U8P_CLOTH::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description text when the player examines the cloth object.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player performs the 'U8P_CLOTH::look' / examine action on the object.

function_map.name: U8P_CLOTH::look
function_map.role: Play a single descriptive bark ('cloth') when the object is examined; then suspend/end the process.
